authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 234, Airline Service Quality Performance Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary regulatory baseline for airline service-quality metric definitions, required reporting, and consumer disclosure constraints.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: metric definition, report assembly, disclosure review
used_for: on-time, late, cancelled, diverted, reportable flight, and reporting-carrier definitions
disclosure and publication boundaries
unsupported public-service-quality claim refusal

authoritative_sources.title: Air Travel Consumer Reports
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Transportation, Office of Aviation Consumer Protection
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official DOT monthly publication describing aviation service-quality reporting sections, source data, cadence, and consumer-facing interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: report assembly, narrative synthesis, disclosure review
used_for: service-quality reporting cadence and source ownership
mishandled baggage, oversales, complaints, and customer-service reporting context
public disclosure caveats

authoritative_sources.title: BTS TranStats Airline On-Time Performance Data
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Transportation, Bureau of Transportation Statistics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official public statistical source for airline on-time performance and delay-cause analytics used to ground metric logic and benchmark claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, metric calculation, benchmark review
used_for: public on-time and delay-cause data source hierarchy
benchmark caveats and denominator discipline
cross-system reconciliation against tenant operational data

authoritative_sources.title: FAA Performance Databases
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FAA source for NAS performance data used to contextualize airport, route, delay, throughput, and service-level analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, route and service-level analysis, disruption review
used_for: NAS performance context
airport and route operational metric caveats
route disruption and service-level impact analysis

authoritative_sources.title: System Wide Information Management (SWIM)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official FAA reference for NAS data exchange and movement-status signals that support operational analytics while preserving ATC and dispatch boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: source collection, movement status validation, route disruption analysis
used_for: movement-status signal expectations
freshness and source lineage for NAS operational data
boundary between analysis and air traffic or dispatch authority

authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 121, Operating Requirements: Domestic, Flag, and Supplemental Operations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary FAA operating-rule baseline for air-carrier dispatch, operational control, maintenance, and safety boundaries that analytics must not override.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: decision boundary, disruption review, safety escalation
used_for: dispatch and operational-control boundary
maintenance and safety escalation triggers
refusal of flight-release or safety-signoff requests

authoritative_sources.title: 19 CFR Part 122, Air Commerce Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary customs baseline for air cargo manifest, advance cargo information, ACAS, clearance, and documentation gates affecting compliant air movement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cargo source validation, movement documentation, customs escalation
used_for: air cargo manifest and advance information requirements
customs documentation gate awareness
refusal of customs-clearance authority

authoritative_sources.title: 49 CFR Part 1548, Indirect Air Carrier Security
authoritative_sources.source_class: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary TSA security baseline for indirect air carrier cargo acceptance, screening, security program, and custody boundaries that affect cargo analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: cargo handoff validation, custody exception handling, security escalation
used_for: cargo screening and security-program boundary
chain-of-custody and handoff failure modes
refusal of screening or security clearance claims

authoritative_sources.title: IATA Ground Operations Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized industry operating-standard source for ground handling, passenger, cargo, mail, load control, GSE, and turnaround terminology.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: terminology normalization, source validation, report assembly
used_for: ground handling and turnaround artifact vocabulary
load control, GSE, passenger, baggage, cargo, and mail workflow terminology
industry-specific metric and evidence expectations

workflow_stages.name: source collection and validation
workflow_stages.description: Collect OOOI, ACARS, DCS, AODB, dispatch, SWIM, FAA, BTS, baggage, cargo, WMS/TMS, customs, screening, maintenance status, and service logs; validate refresh timestamp, event grain, source owner, and lineage.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: metric calculation or report assembly
workflow_stages.description: Define D0, D15, A14, completion factor, cancellation, diversion, turn time, delay cause, baggage, cargo, custody, and service-level metrics with explicit denominators, event anchors, scope, and refresh assumptions.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

workflow_stages.name: narrative synthesis or disclosure review
workflow_stages.description: Translate metric results into audience-ready analysis with caveats, confidence notes, source lineage, and refusal of unsupported causal, benchmark, service-quality, or public-disclosure claims.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: escalation for stale, conflicting, or incomplete data
workflow_stages.description: Block or escalate when AODB, DCS, ACARS/OOOI, dispatch, BTS, SWIM, cargo, customs, screening, or maintenance records are stale, missing, contradictory, or materially incomplete.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references

workflow_stages.name: movement or handoff coordination with chain-of-custody awareness
workflow_stages.description: Analyze movement and handoff signals such as AWB, ULD scan, manifest, screening status, customs document, WMS/TMS, service-level, and station handoff records without taking custody, dispatch, or clearance authority.
source_classes: Chain-of-custody, dispatch, or movement workflow references
Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: schedule disruption, safety, and documentation-gap escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when reroute, diversion, NOTAM, equipment, maintenance, cargo screening, customs, or dispatch-release dependencies threaten compliant or safety-critical movement.
source_classes: Transportation, customs, or safety regulator guidance
Equipment, route, or service-level operating standards

artifacts: AODB stand, gate, and airport movement records
dispatch release, delay code, route, and flight-following records
BTS on-time, delay, cancellation, diversion, baggage, and service-quality records
FAA NAS, SWIM, airport, and route performance data
air waybill, house air waybill, master air waybill, ULD scan, load, and cargo manifest records
ACAS, customs, screening, and compliance documents
WMS, TMS, telematics, chain-of-custody, and handoff records
equipment, maintenance status, MEL/CDL indicator, GSE, and service logs
tenant metric dictionary, source-precedence policy, service-level commitments, cadence, and signoff thresholds
systems_of_record: FAA SWIM and FAA performance data sources
BTS TranStats and DOT Air Travel Consumer Report sources
cargo booking and air waybill system
customer operations and service-quality reporting system

allowed: Classify requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Choose the source hierarchy, metric definitions, denominators, event anchors, refresh assumptions, and caveat posture for an aviation analysis.
Prepare evidence-backed metric summaries, analytical briefs, report packages, reconciliation notes, disclosure-risk notes, and data-quality escalations.
Flag source, denominator, custody, documentation, schedule, safety, or disclosure risk that prevents confident reporting.
Package movement and handoff evidence for downstream operators without authorizing movement.
escalate: Stale, incomplete, inaccessible, or conflicting DCS, ACARS/OOOI, AODB, dispatch, SWIM, BTS, cargo, customs, screening, maintenance, or service records materially change the answer.
Movement, custody, documentation, screening, customs, route, dispatch, equipment, or safety status cannot be established from authoritative records.
Schedule disruption, reroute, diversion, NOTAM, maintenance status, or documentation gap threatens compliant or safety-critical movement.
Tenant policy and public source interpretation conflict or jurisdiction materially changes the workflow.
Publication requires customer-facing, regulator-facing, legal, financial, policy, or safety signoff.
refuse: Refuse flight release, dispatch instructions, route clearance, cargo release, customs clearance, cargo security screening approval, maintenance return-to-service, or safety certification.
Refuse unsupported causal, forecast, benchmark, service-quality, disclosure, or policy claims.
Refuse to hide denominator ambiguity, stale data, unresolved cross-system conflicts, or custody breaks.

domain_failure_modes: Published on-time, delay, cancellation, or completion metric uses a different denominator or event anchor than the named reporting source.
ACARS/OOOI, DCS, AODB, dispatch, BTS, or SWIM records disagree and the analysis collapses them into one unsupported truth.
Cargo AWB, ULD, screening, manifest, WMS/TMS, or customs records break chain-of-custody but reporting claims movement success.
Reroute, diversion, schedule disruption, or airport constraint changes customs, screening, service-level, or safety posture without escalation.
Analytics output implies dispatch, customs, cargo security, ATC, maintenance, or safety approval authority.
Stakeholder asks for public disclosure, causal explanation, benchmark, or stronger conclusion that the evidence does not support.
Tenant-specific metric definition silently replaces DOT/BTS, FAA, or contractual baseline without assumption tracking.
Missing jurisdiction, service model, route, asset, destination, customer commitment, documentation status, or retrieval hook makes the analysis non-comparable.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Route or schedule disruption with compliance impact
Request that implies customs, dispatch, or safety authority
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: non-U.S. jurisdictional metric and clearance rules
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline uses U.S. DOT, FAA, CBP, and TSA sources. Non-U.S. operating jurisdictions must provide local authority sources and tenant source precedence at runtime before publication.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant-specific D0, D15, A14, and controllable-delay definitions
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Tenant-specific metric definitions may be used only when recorded as adaptations and compared against preserved public or contractual baselines.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated U.S.-baseline specification-based package.
Research constrains the pack to analytics, reporting, and disclosure-safe outputs and explicitly excludes dispatch, customs, cargo security, maintenance, ATC, legal, and safety approval authority.
